title: Array and hash literals should be preferred instead of constructors when no parameters are needed
description: This rule raises an issue when Array.new or Hash.new is used without parameters to create empty collections. Using constructor methods like Array.new and Hash.new without parameters is unnecessarily verbose compared to the literal syntax [] and {}. The literal syntax is more concise, widely recognized as idiomatic Ruby, and preferred by the Ruby community.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant:
# Noncompliant: Using constructor without parameters
users = Array.new
config = Hash.new

# Noncompliant: Verbose constructor calls
def initialize
  @items = Array.new
  @settings = Hash.new
end

checks.message: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.
